req.title: Interest accrual
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Savings Account Daily Interest Accrual and Monthly Credit

```python
"""
Test Suite: Savings Account Interest Accrual and Monthly Credit
Requirement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate
             and credit interest monthly on the last business day.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date, datetime
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumption: the production code lives in `savings_account` module.
# Adjust imports to match your actual module structure.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from savings_account import SavingsAccount, InterestEngine, TierRate, BusinessCalendar


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def tier_rates():
    """Standard tier rate schedule."""
    return [
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("0.00"),      max_balance=Decimal("999.99"),    annual_rate=Decimal("0.01")),   # 1%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("1000.00"),   max_balance=Decimal("9999.99"),   annual_rate=Decimal("0.02")),   # 2%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("10000.00"),  max_balance=Decimal("49999.99"),  annual_rate=Decimal("0.03")),   # 3%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("50000.00"),  max_balance=None,                 annual_rate=Decimal("0.04")),   # 4%
    ]


@pytest.fixture
def business_calendar():
    """Business calendar with standard US bank holidays for test year."""
    cal = BusinessCalendar(country="US", year=2024)
    return cal


@pytest.fixture
def account(tier_rates, business_calendar):
    """A standard savings account opened on 2024-01-01."""
    return SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SAV-0001",
        owner="Jane Doe",
        open_date=date(2024, 1, 1),
        initial_balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
        tier_rates=tier_rates,
        business_calendar=business_calendar,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def interest_engine(tier_rates, business_calendar):
    return InterestEngine(tier_rates=tier_rates, business_calendar=business_calendar)


# ===========================================================================
# 1. TierRate Selection
# ===========================================================================

class TestTierRateSelection:

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_lowest_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 1 (< $1,000) should return 1% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("500.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.01")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_second_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 2 ($1,000–$9,999.99) should return 2% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("5000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.02")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_third_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 3 ($10,000–$49,999.99) should return 3% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("25000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.03")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_highest_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance >= $50,000 should return 4% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("100000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.04")

    def test_tier_rate_at_exact_lower_boundary(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance exactly at tier 2 lower boundary ($1,000.00) returns 2%."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("1000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.02")

    def test_tier_rate_at_exact_upper_boundary(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance exactly at tier 2 upper boundary ($9,999.99) returns 2%."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("9999.99"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.02")

    def test_zero_balance_returns_lowest_tier_rate(self, interest_engine):
        """Zero balance should use the lowest tier rate (1%)."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("0.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.01")

    def test_negative_balance_raises_value_error(self, interest_engine):
        """Negative balance is invalid and should raise ValueError."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="balance cannot be negative"):
            interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("-100.00"))


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Daily Interest Accrual Calculation
# ===========================================================================

class TestDailyInterestAccrual:

    def test_daily_accrual_uses_365_day_basis(self, interest_engine):
        """Daily interest = balance × annual_rate / 365."""
        balance = Decimal("10000.00")
        annual_rate = Decimal("0.03")   # tier 3
        expected_daily = (balance * annual_rate / Decimal("365")).quantize(Decimal("0.000001"))
        actual_daily = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(balance, date(2024, 6, 15))
        assert actual_daily == expected_daily

    def test_daily_accrual_non_leap_year(self, interest_engine):
        """Daily basis is 365 for a non-leap year."""
        accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("10000.00"), date(2023, 3, 1))
        expected = Decimal("10000.00") * Decimal("0.03") / Decimal("365")
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_daily_accrual_leap_year_uses_366_day_basis(self, interest_engine):
        """Daily basis is 366 for a leap year (2024)."""
        accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("10000.00"), date(2024, 3, 1))
        expected = Decimal("10000.00") * Decimal("0.03") / Decimal("366")
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_daily_accrual_reflects_tier_change(self, interest_engine):
        """When balance crosses tier boundary, daily accrual reflects new tier."""
        low_balance_accrual  = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("999.99"),  date(2024, 1, 15))
        high_balance_accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("1000.00"), date(2024, 1, 15))
        # Higher tier → higher daily accrual despite tiny balance difference
        assert high_balance_accrual > low_balance_accrual

    def test_daily_accrual_zero_balance_is_zero(self, interest_engine):
        """Account with zero balance should accrue zero interest."""
        accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("0.00"), date(2024, 1, 15))
        assert accrual == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_daily_accrual_accumulates_over_multiple_days(self, account):
        """Accrued interest grows each day it is calculated."""
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        after_day_1 = account.accrued_interest
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 2))
        after_day_2 = account.accrued_interest
        assert after_day_2 > after_day_1

    def test_daily_accrual_does_not_credit_to_principal(self, account):
        """Running daily accrual must NOT increase the account balance."""
        balance_before = account.balance
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        assert account.balance == balance_before

    def test_daily_accrual_not_run_twice_same_day(self, account):
        """Running accrual twice on the same calendar date should raise or be idempotent."""
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        accrual_after_first = account.accrued_interest
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="accrual already run for 2024-06-01"):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        # Accrued interest must not change after duplicate attempt
        assert account.accrued_interest == accrual_after_first

    def test_daily_accrual_uses_end_of_day_balance(self, account):
        """Accrual should be based on the closing balance of the accrual date."""
        account.deposit(Decimal("5000.00"), date(2024, 6, 1))   # raises balance to $10,000
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        # Now in tier 3 (3%) not tier 2 (2%)
        expected_rate = Decimal("0.03")
        expected_accrual = (Decimal("10000.00") * expected_rate / Decimal("366")).quantize(Decimal("0.000001"))
        assert account.accrued_interest == pytest.approx(float(expected_accrual), rel=1e-5)


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Monthly Interest Credit on Last Business Day
# ===========================================================================

class TestMonthlyInterestCredit:

    def test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day_of_month(self, account):
        """Interest must be credited on the last business day (e.g., 2024-01-31)."""
        # January 2024: last calendar day is Wed 31st → last business day is 31st
        for day in range(1, 32):
            accrual_date = date(2024, 1, day)
            account.run_daily_accrual(accrual_date)

        assert account.interest_credited_dates[-1] == date(2024, 1, 31)

    def test_interest_not_credited_before_last_business_day(self, account):
        """No credit event should occur before the last business day of the month."""
        for day in range(1, 30):   # up to but not including last business day
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))

        assert len(account.interest_credited_dates) == 0

    def test_interest_credited_increases_principal_balance(self, account):
        """After credit, account.balance should increase by the credited amount."""
        balance_before = account.balance
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))

        assert account.balance > balance_before

    def test_accrued_interest_resets_to_zero_after_credit(self, account):
        """Accrued interest bucket must be zero after the monthly credit."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))

        assert account.accrued_interest == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_credited_amount_equals_sum_of_daily_accruals(self, account):
        """Total credited amount should equal the sum of all daily accruals for that month."""
        daily_accruals = []
        for day in range(1, 32):
            d = date(2024, 1, day)
            accrual = account.interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(account.balance, d)
            daily_accruals.append(accrual)
            account.run_daily_accrual(d)

        expected_credit = sum(daily_accruals)
        actual_credit   = account.interest_credit_history[date(2024, 1, 31)]
        assert actual_credit == pytest.approx(float(expected_credit), rel=1e-4)

    def test_last_business_day_shifted_when_month_end_is_weekend(self, account):
        """If the last calendar day is a Saturday/Sunday, credit moves to prior Friday."""
        # March 2024: last day is Sunday 31st → last business day is Friday 29th
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 3, day))

        assert date(2024, 3, 29) in account.interest_credited_dates

    def test_last_business_day_shifted_when_month_end_is_holiday(self, account):
        """If the last business day falls on a bank holiday, credit moves to previous business day."""
        # Simulate a custom holiday on 2024-05-31 (Friday)
        account.business_calendar.add_holiday(date(2024, 5, 31))
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 5, day))

        # Previous business day before 2024-05-31 is 2024-05-30 (Thursday)
        assert date(2024, 5, 30) in account.interest_credited_dates

    def test_interest_credited_once_per_month(self, account):
        """Exactly one credit event should occur per month."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))
        for day in range(1, 30):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 2, day))  # Feb has 29 days in 2024

        assert len(account.interest_credited_dates) == 2

    def test_no_credit_if_no_accrual_days_processed(self, account):
        """If no daily accruals are run, the account balance and credit history are unchanged."""
        assert len(account.interest_credited_dates) == 0
        assert account.balance == Decimal("5000.00")

    def test_twelve_monthly_credits_in_full_year(self, account):
        """Exactly 12 credit events should occur across a full calendar year."""
        import calendar

        for month in range(1, 13):
            _, num_days = calendar.monthrange(2024, month)
            for day in range(1, num_days + 1):
                account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, month, day))

        assert len(account.interest_credited_dates) == 12

    def test_credited_interest_compounds_in_subsequent_months(self, account):
        """Credited interest added to principal raises balance, increasing accruals next month."""
        import calendar

        # January accruals
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))
        balance_after_jan = account.balance

        # February accruals – balance is now higher
        total_feb_accrual = Decimal("0.00")
        _, feb_days = calendar.monthrange(2024, 2)
        for day in range(1, feb_days + 1):
            d = date(2024, 2, day)
            total_feb_accrual += account.interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(balance_after_jan, d)
            account.run_daily_accrual(d)

        feb_credit = account.interest_credit_history[date(2024, 2, 29)]
        assert feb_credit == pytest.approx(float(total_feb_accrual), rel=1e-4)
        assert account.balance > balance_after_jan


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Business Calendar
# ===========================================================================

class TestBusinessCalendar:

    def test_weekday_is_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(date(2024, 6, 17)) is True   # Monday

    def test_saturday_is_not_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(date(2024, 6, 15)) is False  # Saturday

    def test_sunday_is_not_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(date(2024, 6, 16)) is False  # Sunday

    def test_us_independence_day_not_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(date(2024, 7, 4)) is False

    def test_us_christmas_day_not_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(date(2024, 12, 25)) is False

    def test_last_business_day_of_january_2024(self, business_calendar):
        """January 31, 2024 is a Wednesday – it is the last business day."""
        assert business_calendar.last_business_day_of_month(2024, 1) == date(2024, 1, 31)

    def test_last_business_day_of_march_2024(self, business_calendar):
        """March 31, 2024 is a Sunday – last business day should be Friday March 29."""
        assert business_calendar.last_business_day_of_month(2024, 3) == date(2024, 3, 29)

    def test_last_business_day_of_november_2024(self, business_calendar):
        """November 30, 2024 is a Saturday – last business day should be Friday November 29."""
        assert business_calendar.last_business_day_of_month(2024, 11) == date(2024, 11, 29)

    def test_custom_holiday_removes_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        """Adding a custom holiday removes that date from business days."""
        target = date(2024, 8, 5)   # Monday – normally a business day
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(target) is True
        business_calendar.add_holiday(target)
        assert business_calendar.is_business_day(target) is False


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_account_opened_mid_month_accrues_from_open_date(self, tier_rates, business_calendar):
        """Account opened mid-month only accrues interest from the opening date."""
        account = SavingsAccount(
            account_id="SAV-0002",
            owner="John Doe",
            open_date=date(2024, 1, 15),
            initial_balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
            tier_rates=tier_rates,
            business_calendar=business_calendar,
        )
        # Try to accrue before open date
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="cannot accrue before account open date"):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, 14))

    def test_accrual_date_in_future_raises_error(self, account):
        """Accrual for a future date should raise ValueError."""
        future_date = date.today() + __import__("datetime").timedelta(days=1)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="accrual date cannot be in the future"):
            account.run_daily_accrual(future_date)

    def test_very_large_balance_accrues_correctly(self, interest_engine):
        """Interest engine handles very large balances without overflow or precision loss."""
        large_balance = Decimal("9999999999.99")
        accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(large_balance, date(2024, 1, 1))
        assert accrual > Decimal("0.00")
        assert isinstance(accrual, Decimal)

    def test_fractional_cent_accruals_are_tracked(self, interest_engine):
        """Sub-cent accruals are stored with sufficient precision (≥ 6 decimal places)."""
        accrual = interest_engine.calculate_daily_accrual(Decimal("1.00"), date(2024, 1, 1))
        # 1.00 * 0.01 / 366 ≈ 0.0000273...
        assert accrual < Decimal("0.01")
        assert accrual > Decimal("0.00")

    def test_february_non_leap_year_last_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        """February 28, 2023 is a Tuesday – it is the last business day of the month."""
        assert business_calendar.last_business_day_of_month(2023, 2) == date(2023, 2, 28)

    def test_february_leap_year_last_business_day(self, business_calendar):
        """February 29, 2024 is a Thursday – it is the last business day of the month."""
        assert business_calendar.last_business_day_of_month(2024, 2) == date(2024, 2, 29)

    def test_interest_credit_record_is_immutable(self, account):
        """Historic credit records cannot be modified after posting."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 1, day))

        credit_amount = account.interest_credit_history[date(2024, 1, 31)]
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, AttributeError)):
            account.interest_credit_history[date(2024, 1, 31)] = Decimal("0.00")

    def test_withdrawal_mid_month_reduces_subsequent_daily_accrual(self, account):
        """A mid-month withdrawal reduces the balance and therefore future daily accruals."""
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        accrual_day1 = account.accrued_interest

        account.withdraw(Decimal("4000.00"), date(2024, 6, 2))  # drops to $1,000
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 2))
        accrual_day2 = account.accrued_interest - accrual_day1

        # Day 1 accrual is at 2% on $5,000; Day 2 accrual is at 2% on $1,000
        assert accrual_day2 < accrual_day1

    def test_deposit_mid_month_increases_subsequent_daily_accrual(self, account):
        """A mid-month deposit increases the balance and therefore future daily accruals."""
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 1))
        accrual_day1 = account.accrued_interest

        account.deposit(Decimal("45000.00"), date(2024, 6, 2))  # moves to tier 4 ($50,000)
        account.run_daily_accrual(date(2024, 6, 2))
        accrual_day2 = account.accrued_interest - accrual_day1

        assert accrual_day2 > accrual_day1
```

---

## Test Coverage Summary

| Area | Tests | Key Scenarios |
|---|---|---|
| **Tier Rate Selection** | 8 | Boundary values, all 4 tiers, zero balance, negative balance |
| **Daily Accrual** | 8 | 365/366-day basis, tier changes, no double-run, EOD balance |
| **Monthly Credit** | 10 | Last biz day, weekend/holiday shift, reset, compounding, 12-month full year |
| **Business Calendar** | 9 | Weekends, public holidays, custom holidays, month-end edge cases |
| **Edge Cases** | 8 | Mid-month open, future date, large balance, leap year, withdrawal/deposit impact |

> **Note:** The tests are written against a proposed interface (`SavingsAccount`, `InterestEngine`, `TierRate`, `BusinessCalendar`). Adjust the imports and method names to match your actual implementation.

# Pytest Tests for Savings Account Daily Interest Accrual and Monthly Credit

```python
"""
Test Suite: Savings Account Interest Accrual and Monthly Credit
Requirement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate
             and credit interest monthly on the last business day.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date, datetime
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumption: the production code lives in `savings_account` module.
# Adjust imports to match your actual module structure.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from savings_account import SavingsAccount, InterestEngine, TierRate, BusinessCalendar


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def tier_rates():
    """Standard tier rate schedule."""
    return [
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("0.00"),      max_balance=Decimal("999.99"),    annual_rate=Decimal("0.01")),   # 1%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("1000.00"),   max_balance=Decimal("9999.99"),   annual_rate=Decimal("0.02")),   # 2%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("10000.00"),  max_balance=Decimal("49999.99"),  annual_rate=Decimal("0.03")),   # 3%
        TierRate(min_balance=Decimal("50000.00"),  max_balance=None,                 annual_rate=Decimal("0.04")),   # 4%
    ]


@pytest.fixture
def business_calendar():
    """Business calendar with standard US bank holidays for test year."""
    cal = BusinessCalendar(country="US", year=2024)
    return cal


@pytest.fixture
def account(tier_rates, business_calendar):
    """A standard savings account opened on 2024-01-01."""
    return SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SAV-0001",
        owner="Jane Doe",
        open_date=date(2024, 1, 1),
        initial_balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
        tier_rates=tier_rates,
        business_calendar=business_calendar,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def interest_engine(tier_rates, business_calendar):
    return InterestEngine(tier_rates=tier_rates, business_calendar=business_calendar)


# ===========================================================================
# 1. TierRate Selection
# ===========================================================================

class TestTierRateSelection:

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_lowest_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 1 (< $1,000) should return 1% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("500.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.01")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_second_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 2 ($1,000–$9,999.99) should return 2% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("5000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.02")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_third_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance in tier 3 ($10,000–$49,999.99) should return 3% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("25000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.03")

    def test_tier_rate_selected_for_highest_tier(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance >= $50,000 should return 4% annual rate."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("100000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.04")

    def test_tier_rate_at_exact_lower_boundary(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance exactly at tier 2 lower boundary ($1,000.00) returns 2%."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("1000.00"))
        assert rate == Decimal("0.02")

    def test_tier_rate_at_exact_upper_boundary(self, interest_engine):
        """Balance exactly at tier 2 upper boundary ($9,999.99) returns 2%."""
        rate = interest_engine.get_applicable_rate(Decimal("9999.99"))
